title: Koanophyllon maestrense
intro: Koanophyllon maestrense, also known as the Maestrense tree, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Koanophyllon maestrense include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Koanophyllon maestrense is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat fever, pain, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Koanophyllon maestrense is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Cuba and belongs to the genus Koanophyllon. Koanophyllon maestrense is classified as a type of daisy or sunflower due to its characteristics and growth habits. The plant is further classified as a member of the tribe Heliantheae. Koanophyllon maestrense is a unique species within the Asteraceae family due to its specific physical and biological traits.
